summaries.134283: Comments confirm `TextField.scrollPadding` is unrelated to the scrollbar position â€” it does not pass padding information to any scrollable part of the TextField, making the parameter name misleading. The external `Scrollbar` widget has no knowledge of the `scrollPadding` value; `RawScrollbar.padding` is the correct API to control scrollbar inset. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open with discussion about whether `ScrollBehavior.buildScrollbar` could be used as a workaround for mobile platforms.
summaries.56176: Issue confirmed reproducible on iOS across multiple Flutter versions up to stable 3.7 and master. Comments clarify the toolbar now stays near the visible portion of the TextField rather than jumping far away, but still differs from native iOS behavior: Flutter always shows the toolbar just above the visible text while native iOS shows it in the middle of the text when the text fills the whole viewport. No PR or fix mentioned; the remaining gap in behavior is documented but unresolved.
summaries.130676: Confirmed reproducible on macOS stable and master. Comments clarify the exception only occurs for Caps Lock (not other keys held at startup) because Caps Lock generates a synthesized KeyUpEvent without a corresponding KeyDownEvent being tracked. The issue is labeled as engine-level. No PR linked; issue remains open.
summaries.28862: Comments identify two distinct sub-problems: `OutlineInputBorder.borderRadius` defaults to `BorderRadius.circular(4)` and must be set on every border state independently (no inheritance from `border`), and large border radius values break the trig code that computes the focused border label gap. PR #93933 improved the label-gap rendering but did not add a `StadiumInputBorder`. A community member provided a standalone `StadiumInputBorder` implementation as a workaround; adding it to the framework is proposed but not yet done.
summaries.89502: Comments confirm this is by-design: `expands: true` requires `maxLines: null`, which conflicts with `obscureText: true` requiring `maxLines: 1`. The original author of the design acknowledged the use case of a tall single-line obscured field was not considered, and discussed potentially allowing `expands` with single-line fields. No fix or PR was proposed; issue remains open.
summaries.138978: Comments confirm the issue on web, macOS, and Android â€” `EditableText` does not support double-tap or drag-to-select because it intentionally omits gesture support; `TextField`/`CupertinoTextField`/`SelectableText` are the full-featured alternatives, or `TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder` can be used to add gesture support manually. Issue #120613 was noted as the tracking issue for web double-tap selection. A workaround using `GestureDetector` with `onDoubleTap` to programmatically select all text was provided.
summaries.162145: Comments confirm the password save dialog regression on iOS between Flutter 3.10.5 and 3.27.1+. Video evidence shows the save dialog appearing in 3.10.5 but not in 3.27.1. The issue is linked to related autofill issues #161576, #148151, #145681, #116889, and #134723, but distinguished as specifically about the save dialog not appearing. Team members cc'd @hhuanlin, @justinmc, and @LongCatIsLooong for investigation; issue remains open with no root cause identified or PR linked.
summaries.41923: Root cause is that `RenderParagraph.assembleSemanticsNode` only supports a fixed set of gesture recognizer types and throws an assertion when encountering `MultiTapGestureRecognizer`. The fundamental fix requires gesture recognizers to set up their own semantics configuration instead of `RenderParagraph` switching over known types (see #40480). Confirmed reproducible through stable 3.5; no fix landed.
summaries.151312: Comments narrow the root cause to iOS only: when Shift is held continuously and Tab is pressed multiple times, the framework receives a synthesized Shift KeyUpEvent even though the key is still physically held down, causing the shortcut resolver to pick `NextFocusAction` instead of `PreviousFocusAction`. Issue is labeled as engine-level and @dkwingsmt was tagged for investigation into why the engine generates a spurious Shift key-up on iOS.
summaries.134398: Comments explain that for non-Android platforms, Flutter prevents coalescing during CJK composition to avoid interference, so `undo` during active composition has no effect and the initial composing state is not saved to the history stack. On Android, a separate embedder-side issue causes `restartInput` to be called after `undo`, adding unexpected history entries and causing blinking. Renzo Olivares had a proof-of-concept fix that caused other regressions; issue is stalled with no active PR.
summaries.35347: Comments confirm that while the issue was not reproducible on web debug builds in some testing, it fails in release web builds. The skipped tests referencing this issue number remain skipped in the test suite. The issue is tracked via skipped tests in `raw_keyboard_test.dart` and `focus_traversal_test.dart`; those tests have not been un-skipped, so the issue remains open.
summaries.99386: Comments confirm Android-only reproduction â€” iOS does not trigger the lifecycle state change. The lifecycle transitions observed are `inactive` then `resumed`, triggered by the Android autofill service gaining and releasing focus when the user selects an autofill suggestion. Issue affects apps that use `didChangeAppLifecycleState` for session management, causing unintended logouts. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open.
summaries.70016: Comments confirm the shifting bug is reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.6 â€” `InputDecorator` moves its child upward when constrained height is less than the text size, causing the top portion to be outside the hit-testable and paintable area. No root cause analysis or fix was proposed in comments beyond confirming reproduction.
summaries.167466: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable 3.29 and master 3.32 on all platforms â€” ellipsis is not shown when text is clipped by a constrained parent height rather than by `maxLines`. One commenter noted it may be working as intended but acknowledged it is hard to work around. A workaround using `LayoutBuilder` and `TextPainter` to compute the number of visible lines dynamically was provided; no framework fix or PR is linked.
summaries.155089: Comments identify the root cause as macOS (and Safari) not sending a `keyup` event for Enter when Meta is held â€” this is a browser/OS behavior, not a Flutter bug. The reporter confirmed the missing `keyup` event in the browser's own `keyup` listener. A Chromium bug was filed at https://issues.chromium.org/issues/366284864. A Flutter-side workaround that simulates a KeyUpEvent after 100ms was provided. Using `SingleActivator` instead of `LogicalKeySet` was suggested as a best practice regardless.
summaries.124493: Comments confirm the regression on Android in Flutter 3.7.6+: Appium/UIAutomator2 can no longer read hint text from TextFields because Flutter intentionally moved the hint text to the `hintText` accessibility property (as opposed to `value`) to fix TalkBack behavior, but UIAutomator2 does not expose `hintText`. The team determined the real fix requires UIAutomator2 to expose `hintText`, and an issue was filed against UIAutomator2. A Flutter-side workaround wrapping `TextField` in a `Semantics` widget with `container: true`, `explicitChildNodes: true`, and a `label` was provided and confirmed to work.
summaries.147020: Comments confirm the bug: after `tester.tap` + `tester.pump`, the `errorText` of `TextFormField` is visible on screen but its semantics label is absent from the semantics tree; it only appears after `pumpAndSettle`. The issue is reproducible on stable 3.19 and master 3.22. No root cause analysis or fix proposed in comments beyond reproduction confirmation.
summaries.125766: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible and explain why a simple fix is not straightforward: `AutovalidateMode.disabled` is the default value for `FormField`, so checking for it to skip validation would break all `FormField` widgets wrapped in a validating `Form`. Comments conclude that adding a new `AutovalidateMode` enum value (to explicitly opt out of parent `Form` validation) is the correct approach. No PR linked; issue is open.
summaries.142138: Issue is confirmed to be Safari-only (Chrome and Firefox do not reproduce). After deleting the text field contents, clicking outside, and clicking back in, keystrokes no longer update the field. A partial improvement was seen on master but the issue was not fully resolved. The reporter applied a JS interop workaround to force HTML-side focus. The issue is noted as related to #137443 which is expected to fix it.
summaries.152547: Comments confirm the issue is iOS-only â€” Android does not reproduce. When switching between text inputs inside a WebView in iOS, the scaffold resizes to avoid the keyboard but then resets to full size, preventing scrolling to the focused input. Comparison with SwiftUI shows native behavior is slightly different but allows scrolling. Issue may be related to #112354. No fix or PR proposed; open.
summaries.95410:
Comments identify the root cause: Flutter's Android text input plugin calls `InputMethodManager.restartInput` whenever the framework changes the composing region, which happens when the built-in single-line input formatter removes `
` characters from the composition. This does not happen in native `EditText` because native editors don't apply such formatters. The issue is specific to `TextInputType.text` (not `emailAddress` or `visiblePassword`). No fix or PR proposed; stalled.
summaries.142897: Comments confirm reproduction on stable and master. Analysis explains that `alignLabelWithHint` was originally designed for vertical alignment only; its horizontal effect on `OutlineInputBorder` was added later via PR #115409 without changing the parameter semantics. Fixing `UnderlineInputBorder` to respect `alignLabelWithHint: false` the same way as `OutlineInputBorder` would require a breaking change because `false` is the default. The proposed solution is a new `InputDecoration` parameter, or subclassing `InputBorder` with `isOutlined: true` to use the outline positioning logic. Issue is stalled; no PR linked.
